homepage.title: James Murray for Ealing North
homepage.text_excerpt: James Murray for Ealing North Skip to main content Homepage My priorities Get in touch About me More Menu James Murray for Ealing North From Ealing North, for Ealing North I grew up off Argyle Road in West Ealing Taking the time to listen I’ve been in touch with or helped local people over 30,000 times I'll always fight to protect our NHS Working hard all year round I set up my office in North Greenford Proud to support local groups across Ealing North Together we can change our country for the better Prev Next I grew up and live in Ealing North and I am honoured to have been re-elected as its MP in 2024 with an increased majority. I want people in Ealing North to be able to afford a decent home, see a doctor when they need one, and have enough money for them and their family. I am very grateful to have the chance to continue serving the people of Ealing North, and to now work with the Labour government to build a better future for us all. Follow on social media Further details Promoted by and on behalf of James Murray, all at Ruskin Hall, 16 Church Rd, London W3 8PP ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: My priorities - James Murray for Ealing North
pages.text_excerpt: My priorities - James Murray for Ealing North Skip to main content Homepage My priorities Get in touch About me More Menu My priorities In 2024 Labour formed a new government after 14 years of the Conservatives. There are no easy solutions but some of my priorities for Ealing North are: Tackle the cost-of-living crisis With economic stability, a growing economy, and keeping taxes, inflation, and mortgages as low as possible. Cut NHS waiting times With more evening and weekend appointments every week, paid for by a crack down on tax avoidance and non-doms. More affordable housing With new council and social housing, and new homes for first-time buyers, alongside a better deal for private renters. Crack down on anti-social behaviour With more neighbourhood police, paid for by cutting wasteful procurement, alongside a new network of youth hubs. Set up ‘Great British Energy’ With our new, publicly-owned clean power company, paid for by a stronger windfall tax, we will cut bills and boost energy security Further details Promoted by and on behalf of James Murray, all at Ruskin Hall, 16 Church Rd, London W3 8PP ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: About me - James Murray for Ealing North
pages.text_excerpt: About me - James Murray for Ealing North Skip to main content Homepage My priorities Get in touch About me More Menu About me Debating in the Chamber About James James was first elected as Labour and Co-operative MP for Ealing North in December 2019. In his first term he served as a member of the House of Commons Health and Social Care Select Committee, an Opposition Whip, and a shadow Treasury minister. James was re-elected in July 2024 and he is currently the Chief Secretary to the Treasury. James at His Majesty's Treasury Before becoming an MP, James Murray was the Deputy Mayor for Housing at City Hall, where he oversaw the Mayor’s £4.8 billion affordable homes programme. This included ‘Building Council Homes for Londoners’ – the first-ever City Hall initiative dedicated to building new council homes – which saw more council homes being built in London than in any time for the last 30 years. New council homes at Copley Close James grew up in Ealing with his brother and mum who was a Labour Councillor in Cleveland (now Pitshanger) from 2014-18. Election Night 2019 Further details Promoted by and on behalf of James Murray, all at Ruskin Hall, 16 Church Rd, London W3 8PP ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
